info.name: TruckFix — kp_backend REST v1 (Fleet + Mechanic prototype mapping)
info.description:
Maps `kp_backend` `/api/v1` routes to the interactive prototype tabs in `FleetApp.tsx` / `MechanicApp.tsx`.

**Seed data** (run `npm run seed:fake` in `kp_backend/`): fleet `fleet@truckfix.dev`, mechanic `mechanic@truckfix.dev`, password `Password123!`. Seeded jobCodes include `TF-8819` (posted + quotes), `TF-8821` (en route), `TF-8823` (awaiting approval).

**Auth**: API returns `{ status, message, data }` — tokens live under `data.accessToken` / `data.refreshToken`.

**Realtime**: job + chat events are Socket.IO (not represented as REST here).

**Admin / Company** endpoints are included as a separate folder with template bodies (not tied to the Fleet/Mechanic prototype nav).

**Variables**: Import `TruckFix.local.postman_environment.json` and **select that environment** in the Postman top-right picker (recommended).
If you leave **No environment**, use **collection variables** (collection → Variables): `baseUrl`, emails, password, and token/job ids after running the auth folder.

**API origin** baked into each request URL: `http://192.168.10.251:6000` (see `DEFAULT_BASE_URL` in `postman/build-truckfix-collection.mjs`). Change it there and run `node postman/build-truckfix-collection.mjs` to regenerate.

**Why the URL bar looked empty before**: requests used only `{{baseUrl}}` in `raw` without a selected environment; some Postman versions show a blank bar until variables resolve.

Login requests store tokens in **both** the active environment and **collection variables**.

request.description: Runs after Fleet login. Saves `jobIdPosted`, `jobIdEnRoute`, `jobIdAwaitingApproval` based on `jobCode*` env vars.

item.name: POST /jobs (create — uses Birmingham-ish coords like seed TF-8819)

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Tyre damage — off-ramp",
  "description": "Off-ramp debris damage — need roadside assessment.",
  "driverName": "Alex Driver",
  "driverPhone": "+27 82 000 0000",
  "issueType": "TYRES",
  "issueSubtype": "FLAT_DAMAGED_TYRE",
  "tyreDetails": {
    "size": "295/80 R22.5",
    "side": "NEAR_SIDE",
    "axlePosition": "Drive 1"
  },
  "urgency": "HIGH",
  "mode": "EMERGENCY",
  "vehicleId": "REPLACE_WITH_VEHICLE_OBJECT_ID",
  "registration": "CA 456-789",
  "vehicleType": "Tautliner",
  "vehicleMake": "DAF",
  "vehicleModel": "XF",
  "estimatedPayout": 120,
  "location": {
    "coordinates": [
      -1.8904,
      52.4862
    ],
    "address": "M6 Motorway, Corley Services, Warwickshire"
  }
}

item.name: POST /fleet/reviews [needs completed job context in real flows]

request.description: In Postman set Body → form-data → key `file` (type File). This export can't embed binary; attach any small JPG/PNG.
Creates a Stripe SetupIntent for the Fleet user. Use the returned publishableKey/clientSecret in a frontend to create a real Stripe PaymentMethod (pm_...).

Attach a Stripe PaymentMethod (pm_...) to the Fleet's Stripe customer, then store it in MongoDB as a PaymentMethod record.

Set `stripePmId` (collection/environment variable) to your Stripe PaymentMethod id.
This request saves the created PaymentMethod `_id` into `fleetStripePaymentMethodDbId` for later use in approve-completion.

item.name: GET /jobs?feed=true (Manchester coords, 15mi — matches seed TF-8821 area)
GET /invoices (mechanic sees assigned invoices per backend rules)
POST /billing/stripe/webhook (raw Stripe payload — not typical Postman)

request.description: Requires Stripe signature + raw body; leave as documentation only.
